channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Prime Day : Amazon brade un ventilateur de plafond Philips avec LED intégré pour souffler pendant la canicule
item.description: Le Philips Civx est un ventilateur de plafond accompagné d'un éclairage LED qui sera du meilleur effet au-dessus de votre lit, à retrouver pour 85,89 euros au lieu de 109,99 euros pour le Prime Day.

item.title: 'Actually, no I cannot hear you Clem Fandango’: these excellent ANC headphones are hugely discounted and yes, you get the voice of Matt Berry
item.description: Cambridge Audio's excellent upgraded Melomania headphones are the bargain of Prime Day.

item.title: Australia's social media ban may not be that effective, study finds
item.description: A study finds that Australia's social media ban hasn't been that effective due to self-declared age verification checks.

item.title: Need a new gaming headset? Don’t miss these stellar Prime Day deals on SteelSeries headsets before they end today
item.description: Don't let Prime Day end before you snatch these stellar SteelSeries gaming headset deals, as the clock is ticking fast.

item.title: With GTA looming, consoles are getting expensive at the worst possible time
item.description: The release of Grand Theft Auto VI is a singular moment, the kind of massive cultural phenomenon that makes people want to go out and buy a console to play it. It is the preeminent modern example of what's known as a "system seller." There's almost certainly a large audience of people who were waiting […]

item.title: Most companies think they're building a software factory. They're actually just shipping bugs faster.
item.description: Industrialized factories changed how the world produced physical goods: more output, lower costs, faster than anything that came before. Now a similar shift is happening with software. LLMs have lowered the barrier to writing code, increased individual output, and pushed organizations to think about software development as a production system. The standard software development lifecycle and CI/CD practices that have held for decades won't hold up under that pressure. That's where the software factory comes in — and like physical factories, it needs more than speed to actually work.The idea of ...

item.title: I found the $3 way to save on roaming charges, dodge slow stadium Wi-Fi speeds and stream all the World Cup matches for free — here's what you need to know
item.description: eSIM, VPN, and a whole lot more to keep you safe while you enjoy the game

item.title: 23 Walmart Deals We Like Better Than That Other Sale Happening Right Now
item.description: Welcome to Walmart deals for folks who’d rather not shop at Amazon. These are the best gadget deals at Walmart this Prime Day.

item.title: Yeti coolers are going cheap for Prime Day — if you know where to look. Here are my top picks as a deal-hunting outdoors expert
item.description: As an outdoors expert, I know where to find all the best deals on hard and soft Yeti coolers — and they're not all at Amazon.

item.title: Samsung will soon start charging to access its smart home API
item.description: From October this year Samsung will roll out a variety of new paid tiers for access to its SmartThings API, including a $4.99 monthly plan for "non-commercial, individual developers." It won't just be developers that pay the price though. Some more advanced smart home users are likely to fall afoul of the rule change if […]

item.title: Prime Day 2026 : cette montre de sport Amazfit en promo à -22% avec écran AMOLED et notée 9/10 tient presque un mois sans recharge
item.description: L'Amazfit Active Max est une montre connectée orientée sport qui propose l'une des meilleures autonomies du marché et qui est à 132,90 euros au lieu de 169,90 euros pour le Prime Day.

item.title: Google Wallet just got two big upgrades to help you track parcels and breeze through airports — here's how to use them
item.description: Google Wallet now supports parcel tracking and TSA PreCheck Touchless ID, taking this well beyond just a wallet app.

item.title: The Morning After: Prices rocket up on Xboxes, MacBooks, iPads and more
item.description: This week, we've been hit with major price hikes from major players, including Microsoft and Apple.

item.title: DJI présente l’Amflow TL Carbon, un VTT électrique tout carbone deux fois moins cher que le précédent
item.description: Amflow, la marque de vélos électriques portée par DJI, dévoile le Amflow TL Carbon, un nouveau VTT électrique équipé du dernier moteur Avinox M2. Il sera commercialisé au prix de 3 499 euros.

item.title: Hurry, the Amazon Ember Mini-LED is a ‘perfect World Cup TV’ and this great Prime Day deal ends very soon
item.description: The Amazon Ember Mini-LED TV is a surprisingly good TV for sports and as it's Prime Day, it's the best time to buy it.

item.title: Apple’s most powerful Macs might be waiting until 2027 for big processor upgrades
item.description: Apple is expected to shake up its usual Mac silicon release strategy, with Bloomberg's Mark Gurman reporting that there won't be Pro or Max versions of the upcoming M6 chip. Instead, Apple wants to "fast-track technologies that it originally planned to release later" with the M7 launch next year. The Cupertino company will reportedly only […]

item.title: New GTA 6 details may confirm players can switch between Jason and Lucia and complete missions as a duo, encounter random world events, and more
item.description: Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders have officially begun, and thanks to Amazon Brazil's listing, new details about the game's open world have emerged.

item.title: NASA's TESS spacecraft finds two 'cotton candy' planets in one system
item.description: NASA's TESS space telescope has discovered two 'super puffy' giant planets with the density of cotton candy.

item.title: Google Wallet pourra bientôt suivre vos colis, en piochant dans vos mails
item.description: Google Wallet sait maintenant suivre vos colis en piochant les infos directement dans Gmail. Mais la fonctionnalité est pour l'instant réservée aux États-Unis.

item.title: You know OpenAI and Nvidia. These are the AI companies building everything else
item.description: The industry’s next phase may depend on the companies building its infrastructure, governance, security, and real-world applications. OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and Nvidia have largely defined the AI narrative for much of the last four years, and for good reason. Nvidia’s chips power virtually every major AI training run in the world. OpenAI’s ChatGPT became the fastest consumer application in history to reach 100 million users. Anthropic has turned Claude into one of enterprise AI’s most trusted model families. Google DeepMind’s AlphaFold solved a protein-folding problem that stumped...

item.title: 3 reasons you should ditch stick vacuums and just buy a corded upright — trust me, I'm a vacuum cleaner tester
item.description: Shark corded uprights are on sale, and there's never been a better time to convert.

item.title: Which MacBook to Buy (2026): Neo, Air, or Pro?
item.description: After testing each of the MacBooks myself, here's my honest advice on which is right for you.

item.title: Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner's Island Resort Could Bring Down Albania’s Prime Minister
item.description: What began as a “flamingo revolution” to protest the $1.4 billion development on Sazan Island has spiraled into mass protests against a ruling party that thousands now want out.

item.title: It’s the last day of Prime Day — here are over 130 great deals to choose from
item.description: We’ve arrived at the final day of Prime Day, which at this point should probably be called “Prime Week.” We’ve found discounts on all manner of gadgets, including TVs, smart home tech, chargers, headphones, and more. Some of the best deals have started selling out at some retailers, so if you’ve been craving a popular […]

item.title: When might Supergirl be released on HBO Max?
item.description: Supergirl has finally soared into theaters worldwide — but when will it be available to watch at home?

item.title: Inside AI’s $5 trillion quest to develop taste
item.description: If agentic commerce is to fulfill its potential, it has to do more than shop for boring, everyday products. Google, OpenAI, the social media giants, and a raft of startups are racing to win the high-stakes, all-too-human race to make AI that has a sense of style. The assignment is charming: I’ve been asked to moderate a panel in a garden in early summer. The problem is that it will require an outfit I don’t own and have no time to find. Easy enough, I think. I use AI every day to summarize transcripts, synthesize financial data, and draft emails. Surely I can outsource this....

item.title: Acheter une voiture électrique reconditionnée, est-ce une option fiable et économique pour vous sans passer par le neuf ?
item.description: Alors que votre budget auto se réduit fortement, le marché de l'occasion électrique se structure autour du reconditionnement industriel et tente de lever les doutes techniques de tous les consommateurs. Voici ce que vous devez savoir sur cette alternative du reconditionné, qui vous permet peut-être de rationaliser vos dépenses.

item.title: I've hand-picked the best last-minute Prime Day deals at Amazon UK — 105+ offers on TVs, appliances, wearables, gaming, and top tech gadgets I'd buy before the sale ends
item.description: With this year's Amazon Prime Day sale set to end at midnight, I've rounded up over 100 of the best deals that are genuinely worth buying before they sell out.

item.title: Qualcomm va nous embrouiller avec 4 puces haut de gamme pour nos smartphones
item.description: Deux nouvelles puces Snapdragon inattendues s'ajoutent au catalogue 2026 de Qualcomm. De quoi multiplier les niveaux de prix sur les smartphones haut de gamme, et brouiller encore un peu plus les noms.

item.title: We're Tracking the Final Hours of Amazon Prime Day Deals Live
item.description: It's Day Four. Somehow it's still Prime Day. The deals are live. The deals are wild. The deals are almost feral. We are still here in the trenches digging up the deals and trends.

item.title: Know your agent: building the foundation of autonomous commerce
item.description: As AI agents become autonomous, establishing cryptographic trust and verifying identity is crucial for business security.

item.title: Prime Day ends soon: We found the 150+ best deals on Apple, DJI, Kindle, and Lego favorites
item.description: Amazon's summer sale is ending soon. Shop the best Prime Day deals on Sony headphones, Samsung TVs, Kindles, and Apple Watches.

item.title: Microsoft extends Windows 10 support out of the blue — consumers now get updates for another year to October 2027
item.description: Worried that you run out of extended support for Windows 10 in a few months? Don't panic — Microsoft is now covering consumers to October 2027.

item.title: Why menopause is employers’ $1.8 billion blind spot—and what leaders can do about it
item.description: Although menopause affects half the world’s population, workplace support remains inconsistent, misunderstood, or absent altogether. As companies work to build more inclusive, high-performing workplaces, one major workforce issue is still hiding in plain sight: menopause. Around 1 billion women globally are currently in perimenopause, menopause, or postmenopause, equating to roughly 12% of the world’s population....

item.title: 3 of the best earbuds for travel — all rated 5-star buys by our Audio Editor
item.description: Including a pair that deliver 'near-impenetrable ANC' for blocking out rowdy fellow travelers.

item.title: 9 Reasons your SEO rankings are up, but traffic is down
item.description: Your positions are climbing. Your traffic is falling. Here is what is actually happening, with the data to prove it.

item.title: Should You Buy Your Kid a 3D Printer? What to Know and Consider (2026)
item.description: Kids love 3D printers almost as much as K-Pop Demon Hunters. Just give in and get one.

item.title: L’iPhone 17 Pro Max chute à un prix inédit pour le dernier jour du Prime Day
item.description: L'iPhone 17 Pro Max est un modèle grand format conçu pour privilégier les performances et l'autonomie. À l'occasion du Prime Day, Amazon applique une baisse de prix inédite sur ce produit particulièrement onéreux : il est actuellement disponible à 1 299 euros au lieu de 1 479 euros.

item.title: The Gooner Music Video Boom Is Here
item.description: Porn music videos have circulated on the fringes of the internet for years. Featuring everything from narrative-driven stories to hypnosis, they are proliferating across X as “bate fuel” for gooners.

item.title: Why your streaming app detects your VPN – and how to avoid it for the World Cup 2026
item.description: Outsmart geo-blocks and secure your connection for uninterrupted World Cup streaming

item.title: Steam Machine is ridiculously expensive — here are 9 PCs (and a $1000 laptop) that easily surpass Valve’s controversial gaming console
item.description: $1,049 for a 512GB customized mini PC with 16GB of RAM and an integrated APU is tough to swallow for many

item.title: Stop Killing the Internet: inside the global movement that wants to save the open web
item.description: As the UK joins the list of countries that want to ban teens from social media, a group of gamers and digital rights activists have come together to prevent the loss of internet freedom, under the guise of protecting children's online safety, and promote evidence-based alternative measures.

item.title: 10 °C en moins en 1 min 40 : Xiaomi lance un smartphone avec ventilateur intégré
item.description: Xiaomi a lancé le Redmi K90 Max Ultimate Edition, un smartphone équipé d'un ventilateur intégré pour mieux gérer la surchauffe. Le modèle promet une réduction de température de 10°C en 1 minute 40 secondes lors d'une utilisation intensive. Il conserve des caractéristiques haut de gamme, telles qu'une batterie de 8000 mAh et une certification IP68/IP69....

item.title: IBM dévoile la première puce sous 1 nm : 100 milliards de transistors sur la taille d’un ongle
item.description: À la surprise générale, IBM est le premier à annoncer un procédé de fabrication dont le nœud technologique est inférieur à 1 nanomètre. Avec son procédé 0,7 nm « Nanostack », le groupe promet de pouvoir installer jusqu'à 100 milliards de transistors sur une puce de la taille d'un ongle.

item.title: Windows 11 : pourquoi la prochaine mise à jour va peser 174 Ko pour certains… et plus de 6 Go pour d’autres
item.description: Microsoft prépare les utilisateurs au futur déploiement de Windows 26H2, une mise à jour qui devrait se faire très discrète pour de nombreux utilisateurs, mais beaucoup moins si cela fait quelques années que vous n'avez pas installé de mise à jour majeure.

item.title: 'Careless People' author accuses Meta of 'punishing' whistleblower
item.description: The author of a tell-all book says Meta is using her as an example to deter future whistleblowers.

item.title: White House wants OpenAI to limit the launch of its next model
item.description: The Trump administration reportedly wants OpenAI to slow roll the launch of its next model.

item.title: Amazon casse encore plus les prix pour le dernier jour de son Prime Day : voici les ultimes offres en DIRECT
item.description: Dernières heures pour le Prime Day d'Amazon ! L'événement touche à sa fin aujourd'hui et c’est l’ultime occasion de faire de bonnes affaires. Découvrez notre sélection des meilleures offres mise à jour en temps réel pour dénicher les vrais bons plans avant qu'il ne soit trop tard.

item.title: I Found The Best Amazon Prime Day Headphone Deals (2026)
item.description: From AirPods to on-ears, we’ve tested hundreds of pairs of headphones. Here are the best deals from Amazon’s biggest sale event.

item.title: Ed Norton watched Team USA at the World Cup and everyone made the same joke
item.description: Ed Norton was photographed at the World Cup with Brad Pitt, and everyone made the same joke on social media.

item.title: Vers une nouvelle pénurie de console PS5 et Xbox à cause de la crise et GTA 6
item.description: Entre la crise de la RAM qui met sous tension tout le secteur de la tech et le succès de GTA 6, les commerçants craignent une rupture de stock des consoles à la fin de l'année.
